text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off, semis diverged, oil surged. The day was a headline-driven mess: oil spiked +3% on the Iran deal collapse, dragging energy stocks higher but crushing broad equities, with tech’s SOX rebounding +1.87% as the only bright spot.

INDICES — VIX at 16.9 below its 20-day MA. Rankings: SOXX +1.87%, QQQ +0.28%, SPY -0.31%, IWM -0.91%, DIA -1.07%. Large > small by 1.19pp. Most notable cross-asset: USO +3.02%. Neither SPY nor QQQ is below the 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio at 0.9x, QQQ at 0.8x — both below average, with price down for SPY and up for QQQ, signaling low-conviction moves. Elevated volume on XLY at 2.1x with price down -1.78% signals distribution.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +1.76%, XLK Technology +1.24%, XLP Staples -0.55%, XLU Utilities -0.74%, XLI Industrials -1.07%, XLV Healthcare -1.30%, XLC Comms -1.41%, XLRE Real Estate -1.65%, XLY Discretionary -1.78%, XLF Financials -1.93%, XLB Materials -2.62%. Top industries: WGMI +4.75%, KWEB +3.53%. Bottom: XHB -3.08%, SILJ -3.40%. Money rotated into energy and chipmakers, out of cyclicals and miners — call it a defensive hedging rotation.

POSITIONING — QQQ put-call ratio at 1.38 shows heavy protection demand, while IV skew at +13.2% confirms fear. QQQ spot at 610 sits near the gamma flip, increasing dealer hedging pressure.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil surging +3% while SPY falls -0.31% — classic stagflation signal. The Iran deal break is the catalyst pressuring equities, with energy the only benefiting group.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.5 Greed. Top movers: USO +3.02% (vol spike 2.6x — accumulation, energy), KWEB +3.53% (vol spike 2.4x — accumulation, China internet), XLY -1.78% (distribution, discretionary). Watch tomorrow: XHB -3.08%, today’s worst industry on oil fear; KWEB +3.53%, with massive vol spike signaling momentum break; XLF -1.93%, the heaviest cyclical drag and a potential gamma trap.
